reason: Node 1.2 claims '1 is an order unit for (A,A_+)', but per the byte-verified GT-orderunit-def (HOS 1.2.1, refs/hos/joa-m.md:368: 'An element e in A^+ is called an order unit for A if for all a in A there is lambda>0 ...'), being an order unit is a TWO-clause predicate: (i) the cone-membership e in A_+, AND (ii) the dominance condition. Node 1.2's body derives ONLY clause (ii) (the inequalities -||a||1 <= a <= ||a||1). It never derives clause (i), 1 in A_+. This is a genuine logical gap, not a cosmetic one, because: (a) it is part of the very HOS definition being claimed; and (b) it is load-bearing downstream -- node 1.4(ii) explicitly cites THIS node for '1 in A_+ and 1 is an order unit for A by node 1.2', so node 1.4 attributes 1 in A_+ to node 1.2 even though 1.2 omits its derivation. The fact is TRUE and derivable from material 1.2 already cites (1 in A by node 1.1; 1 in V_+ because the order unit of the order-unit space V lies in V_+ by GT-orderunit-def + GT-jb-orderunit-3310; hence 1 in A cap V_+ = A_+), so the fix is a one-line addition. Given the granularity bar set by the validated sibling 1.1 (which explicitly justified '1=P(1) in Im P=A, giving 1 in A'), node 1.2 must explicitly derive 1 in A_+ before claiming the full order-unit predicate. FIX: add a sentence deriving 1 in A_+ = A cap V_+ from 1 in A (node 1.1) and 1 in V_+ (GT-orderunit-def + GT-jb-orderunit-3310).
